---
description: "Use when adding or editing i18n translations, working with locale JSON files, or using t() in Vue templates. Covers key naming, file location, and markup conventions for vue-i18n."
applyTo: "**/locales/*.json, **/views/*.vue, **/components/**/*.vue"
---

# i18n Guidelines

## Locale File Location

Translation files live in `src/locales/` within each project.
- `en.json` is the **master language file** — always add keys here first.
- German (`de.json`) is supported in most projects; other languages are community-contributed.

## Key Structure

Use a **hierarchical key structure** that mirrors the view or component name where the text appears.

- Keys for a view (`SetupBot.vue`) live under `setupBot.*`
- Keys for a reusable component (`BotModeSelection.vue`) live under `botModeSelection.*` at the top level (not nested under a view), since they are shared across views
- Group related keys in nested objects rather than flattening them

```json
{
  "setupBot": {
    "title": "Setup Opponent",
    "instructions": {
      "intro": "Set up a complete two-player game, with the following changes:",
      "ladyPeiNoStartingResources": "Your opponent, Lady Pei, doesn't receive any starting money or Pots.",
      "youAreFirstPlayer": "You are always the first player. You receive ${startingMoney} as starting money."
    }
  },
  "botModeSelection": {
    "title": "Solo Mode",
    "base": "Base Mode",
    "advanced": "Advanced Mode"
  }
}
```

## Named Keys

Always use **descriptive, named keys** that reflect the meaning of the text — never generic positional keys like `text1`, `text2`, `line1`, `line2`, `item1`, etc.

```json
// WRONG
"line1": "Your opponent does not receive starting money.",
"line2": "You are always the first player."

// CORRECT
"ladyPeiNoStartingResources": "Your opponent does not receive starting money.",
"youAreFirstPlayer": "You are always the first player."
```

## Markup in Templates

Always use `v-html` for `<p>` and `<li>` elements so that translations can contain inline HTML (e.g. `<strong>`, `<a>`, `<b>`).

```vue
<!-- WRONG -->
<p>{{ t('setupBot.instructions.intro') }}</p>
<li>{{ t('setupBot.instructions.youAreFirstPlayer', {startingMoney}) }}</li>

<!-- CORRECT -->
<p v-html="t('setupBot.instructions.intro')"/>
<li v-html="t('setupBot.instructions.youAreFirstPlayer', {startingMoney})"/>
```

For headings and other inline text where HTML is not expected, `{{ t('...') }}` is acceptable.

## Interpolation

Use **named placeholders** with `${}` syntax in JSON values and pass an object in `t()`:

```json
"youAreFirstPlayer": "You receive ${startingMoney} as starting money."
```

```vue
<li v-html="t('setupBot.instructions.youAreFirstPlayer', {startingMoney})"/>
```
